rows.answer:
Fixed. `.merge()` now throws on a refined receiver and preserves refinements from the incoming schema, matching how upstream zod resolved this exact issue ([#5842](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5842), fixed by [PR #5856](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5856)).

**Root cause:** In `packages/zod/src/v4/core/util.ts`, `merge()` built its new def with `checks: []`, unconditionally discarding refinements — from the receiver `a` explicitly, and from `b` implicitly (only `b`'s shape and catchall were copied over). When [#5317](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5317) made `.pick()`/`.omit()` (and `.partial()`) throw on refined object schemas, `merge` was missed.

**The fix** (`util.ts:687`), mirroring the merged upstream PR exactly:
- If `a._zod.def.checks?.length`, throw `".merge() cannot be used on object schemas containing refinements. Use .safeExtend() instead."` — consistent with pick/omit/partial.
- Changed `checks: []` to `checks: b._zod.def.checks ?? []`. The asymmetry is deliberate: `b` is the "incoming" schema (its catchall wins too), and every field its refinements reference exists in the merged shape, so keeping them is sound — whereas keeping `a`'s refinements after `b` may have overwritten fields would be unsound, hence the throw.

**Verification (all ran, all passed):**
- My repro script: refined receiver throws; a refinement on the incoming schema now rejects `{ age: 5, name: "test" }` with "Must be 18+" and accepts valid input; plain merge still works.
- Full `zod` project suite: **326 test files, 3,688 tests passed, no type errors** (exit code 0), so nothing relied on the old silent-drop behavior.

One thing to note: zod-mini's `z.merge()` routes through `util.extend`, which has its own policy (throws only when overwriting refined keys) — upstream left that path untouched, so I did too.

Sources: [Issue #5842](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5842), [PR #5856](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/5856)
